differentials.name: Intestinal obstruction Mortality rate:   10% mortality if isolated abnormality; 80% with one / more concurrent malformations; ~ 100% with chromosomal + cardiovascular abnormalities

differentials.raw: Physiologic herniation of midgut into umbilical cord between 8th and 12th week of gestation

context: intestinal atresia (vascular compromise); malrotation; abnormal fixation of liver; esophageal atresia; facial cleft; diaphragmatic hernia Sac contents:   primarily bowel, liver, other viscera (variable) √  herniation of abdominal viscera at base of umbilical cord: liver (27%) ± stomach ± bowel √  midline central defect at base of umbilical cord insertion: √  defect over entire ventral abdominal wall of variable size (mean, 2.5-5 cm) √  widened cord where it joins the skin of the abdomen √  covering amnioperitoneal membrane: √  inner layer = peritoneum; outer layer = amnion √  cord inserts at a
